req.title: Lab order routing
req.statement: A clinician shall order a lab test; orders shall route to the patient's preferred lab when available.
error_pathways: fallback to default lab when preferred unavailable

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three distinct routing scenarios: (1) preferred lab is available → order routes there (EP positive path); (2) preferred lab is unavailable → fallback to default lab (DT/NEG error pathway); (3) boundary condition where preferred lab transitions from available to unavailable mid-order cycle (STC/BVA). A fourth Decision-Table case covers the clinician submitting a lab order with no preferred lab configured, ensuring default routing is deterministic. Together these four cases satisfy the mandated minimum of positive, negative, and boundary categories while exercising all cited heuristics.

tests.name: test_lab_order_routes_to_preferred_lab_when_available
preconditions: Patient has a preferred lab configured (lab_id='LAB-PREFERRED')
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with subject patient and intent=order
Include performer reference pointing to preferred lab
expected: Order tracking entry created with routed_lab='LAB-PREFERRED'
trace: R-HLT-018: order routed to preferred lab when available

tests.name: test_lab_order_falls_back_to_default_lab_when_preferred_unavailable
preconditions: Patient has a preferred lab configured (lab_id='LAB-PREFERRED')
actions: Set preferred lab status to INACTIVE via test seeding
POST /ServiceRequest with subject patient and performer referencing preferred lab
expected: Order tracking entry records routed_lab='LAB-DEFAULT' and fallback_reason='preferred_unavailable'
trace: R-HLT-018: fallback to default lab when preferred unavailable
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

@pytest.fixture
def clinician_headers():
    return <fixture: clinician_auth_headers>

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def cleanup_service_requests():
    created = []
    yield created
    for rid in created:
        requests.delete(f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest/{rid}", headers=<fixture: admin_headers>)

def test_lab_order_falls_back_to_default_lab_when_preferred_unavailable(clinician_headers, cleanup_service_requests):
    patient_id = <helper_fn: create_patient_with_preferred_lab("LAB-PREFERRED")>
    <helper_fn: set_lab_status("LAB-PREFERRED", "inactive")>
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "performer": [{"reference": "Organization/LAB-PREFERRED"}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "2093-3"}]}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest", json=payload, headers=clinician_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    cleanup_service_requests.append(body["id"])
    assert body["performer"][0]["reference"] == "Organization/LAB-DEFAULT"
    tracking = <helper_fn: get_order_tracking(body["id"])>
    assert tracking["routed_lab"] == "LAB-DEFAULT"
    assert tracking["fallback_reason"] == "preferred_unavailable"

tests.name: test_lab_order_boundary_preferred_lab_becomes_unavailable_between_requests
preconditions: Patient has preferred lab (lab_id='LAB-PREFERRED') initially ACTIVE
actions: POST first /ServiceRequest while preferred lab is ACTIVE
Immediately set preferred lab to INACTIVE (boundary state flip)
POST second /ServiceRequest for the same patient
expected: Both orders have distinct IDs and tracked statuses
No data from first order contaminates second order routing
trace: R-HLT-018: BVA on preferred-lab availability state boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

@pytest.fixture
def clinician_headers():
    return <fixture: clinician_auth_headers>

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def cleanup_service_requests():
    created = []
    yield created
    for rid in created:
        requests.delete(f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest/{rid}", headers=<fixture: admin_headers>)

def test_lab_order_boundary_preferred_lab_becomes_unavailable_between_requests(clinician_headers, cleanup_service_requests):
    patient_id = <helper_fn: create_patient_with_preferred_lab("LAB-PREFERRED")>
    <helper_fn: set_lab_status("LAB-PREFERRED", "active")>
    order_payload = lambda: {
        "resourceType": "ServiceRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "performer": [{"reference": "Organization/LAB-PREFERRED"}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "2093-3"}]}
    }
    r1 = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest", json=order_payload(), headers=clinician_headers)
    assert r1.status_code == 201
    b1 = r1.json()
    cleanup_service_requests.append(b1["id"])
    assert b1["performer"][0]["reference"] == "Organization/LAB-PREFERRED"
    <helper_fn: set_lab_status("LAB-PREFERRED", "inactive")>
    r2 = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/ServiceRequest", json=order_payload(), headers=clinician_headers)
    assert r2.status_code == 201
    b2 = r2.json()
    cleanup_service_requests.append(b2["id"])
    assert b2["performer"][0]["reference"] == "Organization/LAB-DEFAULT"
    assert b1["id"] != b2["id"]

tests.name: test_lab_order_with_no_preferred_lab_routes_to_default
preconditions: Patient has NO preferred lab configured
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with subject patient who has no preferred lab
Omit performer field to let system resolve routing
expected: No fallback_reason field set (default was primary choice)
trace: R-HLT-018: DT row — preferred lab absent, default used
